TextENGNew: After hanging up with Shion, I search the school's contact list for Rika-chan's phone number.
...Found it!
My fingers tremble with panic, and I keep failing to dial even that mere five-digit number.
Keiichi... Calling at this hour? It's rude to phone people so late at night, so stop...
I-it's not the time for that!!
I bark at my mother and listen hard to the ringing tone. ...Pick up... please, pick up...!!
The time... just before 11 PM.
It certainly isn't early. It's the kind of hour that should be called deep night.
...Has Rika-chan already gone to sleep...?
But with it ringing this much... won't it wake her up?!
Rika-chan... doesn't answer.
......No answer.
Maybe she just can't hear it.
...Maybe her bed is far from the phone... no, that can't be it...
...If you're asleep, a phone ringing should sound all the clearer.
......Then maybe... the restroom... no, the bath!
If she's bathing, even if she hears the phone ringing, she can't pick it up...
...It's a little late for a bath, but it's possible Rika-chan has a habit of bathing before bed.
.....................I'll wait about thirty minutes, then call again...
Even as I thought that, I called Rika-chan's house again and again.
I kept calling.
Over and over, again and again, I kept calling.
...But no one answers.
I think I kept calling for about thirty minutes. ...And still, no answer.
Are the calls so relentless that she got scared and can't even pick up the phone?!
...No, that can't be...
There's no point in this anymore.
...Going there myself and checking is the surest way...!
But... where is Rika-chan's house...?
Damn it... I've never even asked where it is...!!
...Her address is on the contact list, but it's written as a block number, so even looking at it, I can't tell where it is.
...Wouldn't it be on a map or something?!
Furude Rika... Her surname is Furude.
It's an unusual surname. I might find it pretty easily...!!
Thinking that, I rummaged through the phone drawer, but all that came out were things like takeout menus and public office numbers...!
Damn, damn... damn...!!
I kept dumping out everything in the drawer one after another... but I couldn't find a single clue to where Rika-chan's house was.
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi...!
If you don't know... then ask! Ask someone who does!
...Right... Rena has to know!
I pulled Rena's phone number from the contact list.
...Ryuuguu, Rena... there it is.
...Hello. This is Ryuuguu.
A gruff, ill-tempered older man's voice.
...That's Rena's dad. It's a late-night call, so I'd better be extra polite...
Y-yes... I-I'm sorry to call so late at night. My name is Maebara, is Rena-san there...?
Rena is in the bath, but... ah, maybe she's out? ...Rena. You've got a call from a friend.
Right, Ryuuguu Reina was her real name.
...I'd grown so used to Rena that her real name, Reina, feels stranger.
Hello. Rena speaking.
Ah... i-it's me, Keiichi. Sorry for calling so late...!
Huh? Keiichi-kun? What is it at this hour, I wonder? I wonder?
...Actually, um... I want to know where Rika-chan's house is.
Rena sounded a little exasperated, like for a call this late, that was all it was...
But maybe she sensed the urgency anyway, because her tone turned sharply serious.
U-um, yeah. That's fine. You know the meeting hall on the grounds of Furude Shrine, right? Do you know the two-story storage shed behind it? That's where she lives.
...I couldn't picture it right away, but if I at least knew she lived on the shrine grounds, I could find it somehow.
O-okay, got it! Thanks. Sorry for calling this late. Apologize to your dad for me.
That's fine, Keiichi-kun. More importantly, why are you asking where Rika-chan lives?
With a sharpness she never showed in ordinary times, Rena pressed me before I could even hang up.
...For a moment, I hesitated to tell Rena.
Because I told... I was killed.
...Keiichi-kun? If you can hear me, I want you to answer. Why do you want to know where Rika-chan's house is at this hour?
Rena was putting on her usual tone, but beneath her words was a taut urgency, keenly aware that this was an emergency.
...What do you do, Keiichi?
If it's Rena... maybe I can tell her...?
Because I found out... I was killed.
Because I confessed... ...I was killed.
...Keiichi-kun?! Rena is asking you seriously. I want you to answer!
Rena asked again in a forceful tone I could never have imagined from her. ...That intensity startled me.
...Rena is Rika-chan's friend too.
...She has the right to worry about Rika-chan's disappearance...
...If I tell her, then Rena too...
...I crush that terrifying thought down... just for now!
...Rena... um... I'd be in trouble if you asked me why...
Yeah.
Rena's voice was utterly serious.
...It even sounded like she was saying, No matter what Keiichi-kun says, I'll believe him.
...That little bit of reassurance gave me the courage to open up...
Rika-chan... I feel like she's in danger.
...Is it a premonition? ...Or do you have some certainty that danger is closing in on Rika-chan...?
...Honestly, I couldn't even spare the time to stand here discussing this with Rena.
...But listening to Rena's calm voice, I felt my useless panic draining away.
...I don't... have certainty, but...
Even so, I still couldn't get the words out.
...I'd told Rika-chan that we snuck into the Saiguden.
changeReason: Changed between 'Saiguden' (Japanese term) and 'ritual storehouse' (English translation) for 祭具殿

TextENGNew: The village chief, the one we'd told first, disappeared last night.
So... Rika-chan could be next...
...I didn't know how to explain it to Rena.
...While I was still stumbling over my words, Rena spoke.
Sorry for asking something weird. Now that I think about it, we didn't need certainty at all.
Rena laughed lightly, open and matter-of-fact.
...I wasn't in any state to laugh, so I was the one left dumbfounded.
Rena continued in a bright voice, but with firm conviction.
When you're worried about a friend, I don't think you need a reason to check on them.
...Even if Rika-chan was asleep and we ended up waking her, if that's why, I don't think any of us would be mad.
...Rena... thank you...

Then first, let me confirm with you, Keiichi-kun.
...You called Rika-chan's house, right?
And since you absolutely couldn't get through, you came to ask where her house is, right?
Ah... y-yeah! I think it kept ringing for more than ten minutes. ...Maybe they just didn't notice and are still asleep...
Rika-chan's house is really small. So if the phone rang, there's no way they wouldn't notice, even if they were asleep. Satoko-chan is there too, so there's no way both of them would miss it.
...Huh? Satoko lives together with Rika-chan...?
Yeah. You didn't know? ...We'll talk about that later! ...Anyway, if it rang that long and nobody picked up, that's definitely strange.
Rena understood far faster than I'd imagined that something was wrong.
That made her feel dependable... but the fact that even Rena admitted this was abnormal also meant the best, brightest possibility, that this was all just my needless worry, was gone.
I'm going to Rika-chan's house right now too. Want to go together? Then I'll come pick you up.
Got it. Then let's meet at our usual spot! I'll wait there!!
Nn-no! I'm coming to pick you up at your house, Keiichi-kun. So you can just wait at home. And tell your family you're going with me to Rika-chan's house!
...At my house? Really? That feels like a waste of time...
Also, did you call Mii-chan? She's super dependable at times like this! If you haven't, I'll call her myself!!
For an instant, my thoughts froze.
...Because in the call I'd just had with Shion, she'd told me I should be careful around Mion.
...If the Sonozaki main house was moving in the shadows behind the serial mysterious deaths that happened every year...
...then Mion, as heir to the family head, couldn't be unrelated...
Then... the mysterious deaths of Takano-san and the others this year, and the village chief's disappearance, weren't unrelated either...?
See you! I'll be there right away! Wait for me!!
After saying only that, Rena hurriedly hung up.
...What I needed now wasn't to suspect Mion.
It was to confirm that Rika-chan was safe.
I changed clothes and got my bicycle key ready.
I thought it'd be smoother if we met at the place where we usually met on school mornings.
...There was no need to make Rena come all the way to my house.
When I told my parents I was going out, they seemed ready to make a fuss, so I stepped outside without a word.
It was a night without its usual coolness, thick with an unpleasant dampness.
...They did say Rika-chan and Satoko live together.
Then... does that mean it's not just Rika-chan... Satoko is gone too?!
The meaning of those two living together instantly turned into the worst possible thought.
No... Satoko is... she really has nothing to do with this, right?!
Rika-chan may have had a reason to disappear... but Satoko shouldn't have had one...!!
K-Keiichi-kun...!!!
Rena was really fast.
She came racing over on her bike.
...Her ragged breathing vividly showed just how much of a hurry she had been in.
Hey, Rena! Come on, let's go!!
...Keiichi-kun. I told you to wait at home.
Rena said it with a serious look.
...Is she angry? I had no idea why, and for a moment I was confused...
N-no... I just thought we'd be able to leave faster if we met up here...
...Keiichi-kun... do you really understand?!
Rena shouted, her anger plain to see. ...It was the first time I'd ever seen her like this.
W-what do you mean, understand... understand what...
Rena seemed to realize she was frightening me, and after taking one or two deep breaths, she spoke as if to admonish me... but her expression never softened.
Keiichi-kun. Yesterday, the village chief disappeared and the whole village was in an uproar, right?
And in the middle of that, you're saying Rika-chan and Satoko-chan have disappeared.
...Yeah... S-so I guess that's what it means...
Then you have to be more careful!!! Standing around here all alone in the middle of the night is far too careless!!
...I gradually began to understand the meaning of Rena's anger...

Your family knows, right? That you're going to my house and Rika-chan's? ...Just in case. ...Even if Keiichi-kun and I both end up disappearing, they'll still have a record of when, and roughly where, we vanished.
...Rena was eerily calm, clear-headed even in this emergency.
Since that night of Watanagashi...
Every night, someone had died or disappeared.
On the night of Watanagashi, Takano-san and the others met unnatural deaths.
...The next night, the village chief disappeared.
...And the night after that, Rika-chan and Satoko disappeared...
...I'd only been thinking about myself, but right now Hinamizawa was trapped in an extreme abnormal state, so severe I wasn't even sure the word abnormal was enough anymore...
Rena had thought that far ahead, even to the possibility of our own disappearance, and told me to leave word with my family about where we were going.
...I was ashamed of my own carelessness, and afraid of this night that made that shame inescapable.
...Sorry. ...I still hadn't told them.
Yeah. Then let's go tell them together!
I turned right around and rushed back home with Rena.
...The moon was unnaturally high.
...The sky was needlessly vast and bitterly cold, already forcing a cruel truth on us: whatever happened, we would never be able to call it a dream.

On this mad night, Rena's steadiness, far beyond anything I'd ever have imagined from her, was reassuring... and more frightening than anything was what Hinamizawa had become, enough that even Rena had to warn me for being careless...
The moon was unnaturally high.
Mion, who met up with us on the way, was naturally only half-convinced about Rika-chan and the others disappearing.
...Kei-chan, are you serious? If this is a joke, I'm going to be really mad.
Mion was clearly in a foul mood. If this was a joke, it was far too tasteless to laugh at.
...I knew exactly how inappropriate what I was saying was.
Since Watanagashi the night before last, people had died or disappeared every night.
...And after that, saying even Rika-chan and Satoko had disappeared was something you could never, ever joke about.
Mii-chan. If it really were a joke, don't get mad, let's laugh. Because it'd be a joke.
...We have to make sure it really is a joke.
.........Yeah. ...Sorry for sounding so irritable.
No matter how tasteless the joke, if it ends with everyone safe, it's nothing more than something to laugh about... Reminded of that by Rena, Mion let some of her tension go and gave a wry smile.
Mion swung onto her bike too.
On that pitch-black road with barely any streetlights, the lights of our three bicycles cut through the darkness.
Kei-chan, why did you suddenly feel like calling Rika-chan's house at this hour?
...Mion asked again about the thing I hadn't been able to explain well even to Rena.
......A premonition... would that sound strange?
It was too dark to make out clearly, but Mion was smiling with an uncertain look.
...Even in this darkness, it was clear she wasn't fully convinced.
You know, when I have a bad dream while dozing off, I just can't stop worrying about it.
Keiichi-kun... so you dreamed Rika-chan was kidnapped...?
......No... um... It was something vaguer... more like a premonition. ...If Rika-chan had picked up, I was going to call Rena and Mion next.
The lie came out smoothly, almost too smoothly for something I made up on the spot.
If I talked about my connection to Shion, it'd be like indirectly admitting we snuck into the Saiguden together that night...
changeReason: Changed between 'Saiguden' (Japanese term) and 'ritual storehouse' (English translation) for 祭具殿

TextENGNew: Mion didn't ask any further.
...I don't know if she accepted it.
Or maybe she thought it was quicker to confirm Rika-chan and Satoko were safe than worry about that.
Feeling relieved by that somehow made me feel guilty...
......These past few days, Mion hadn't really changed how she acted, and yet... for some reason, I kept trying to keep my distance.
Even though all Shion and Ooishi-san told me was that Mion was the heir to some grand family...
.........Was I just trying to force myself to forget?
Remember, Maebara Keiichi.
...Wasn't Mion the very first one to ask about the night of Watanagashi?
...And in such a harsh tone, too. Had I already forgotten how I froze up then...?
...That Mion... definitely looked different from the Mion I usually know.
...If we're talking only about that Mion, ...she did overlap, just a little, with the image of Mion that Shion and Ooishi-san describe...
That's right.
...If not for the Mion from that day, ...I would never have felt this way about Mion.
...Just what was that Mion that day...?
...By conveniently filling my head with all the things that happened afterward, ...am I turning my eyes away from the very first thing, the one thing I should never ignore...?
Mion pedaled ahead of me, her long hair fluttering.
...No matter how long I stared at her back, the answers to those questions never came to me...
We came to the foot of the stone steps leading into the shrine grounds.
Apparently no one intended to carry a bicycle up. Everyone parked their bikes beside the stone steps.
...Furude Shrine, huh.
Everyone comes and goes like it's a park, but this is actually the Furude family's private property.
The whole shrine is private land, huh.
...This shrine really does feel old.
...It looks like it's been here far longer than just ten or twenty years.
...If that's the case, doesn't that make the Furude family quite an old house? A lineage with history...
..................Yeah. There aren't any. That's strange.
Rena said something clearly unsettling, so I hurried over to her.
What's wrong, Rena?
...Rika-chan and Satoko-chan always park their bikes here. ...See? They're nowhere.
I looked all around us.
...Sure enough, there were no bicycles other than the ones we rode here.
Isn't it possible they parked somewhere else?
...Like, maybe they carried them up the stone steps?
That's impossible for girls, Keiichi-kun.
Certainly, it was hard to imagine Rika-chan and Satoko hauling their bicycles up these stone steps.
...Before we even knocked on Rika-chan's door, reality was already shoving grim possibilities in our faces.
...Maybe they were just parked in some patch of shade we hadn't noticed.
Not finding the bicycles here and now couldn't possibly count as proof they were missing...
Let's go...! We'll know a lot more for sure if we head to the house!
The three of us nodded to each other and sprinted up the stone steps.
We passed under the torii and entered the shrine grounds, neatly spread with gravel.
...It was so silent it felt like a lie that the Watanagashi festival had ever been held here.
...So where's the house where Rika-chan and the others live?
This way. Follow me.
Rena took point and ran.
...She headed toward the meeting hall at the side of the grounds, then around to the back.
In the pitch dark stood a two-story prefab hut, like some kind of storage shed.
...The lights are off... Maybe they're asleep?
Let's try knocking.
Rena and Mion approached the prefab hut, which hardly looked like a place anyone lived in.
...With a shrine this grand, I'd assumed they lived in a much finer house. ...It was a real surprise.
Rika-chaaaan!!! Satoko-chaaaaan!! Are you theeeere?!
Rena called up toward the second floor. At first she was reserved, then gradually louder.
...More than that, there wasn't even a hint of anyone inside.
...Maybe they really are asleep. Let's wake them up.
Mion pounded both hands against the shutter, and the noisy thuds echoed all around us.
...With this much racket, they had to notice.
...They should've turned on the light, flung the window open, and shouted, do you have any idea what time it is?!
......But that doesn't happen. ......There was no response at all.
Once Mion stopped banging on the shutter, a sudden silence came crashing in.
...That silence... stirred up my worst fears...
...I could feel the blood draining from my face in a rush...
It's locked... Maybe we can get in from somewhere...
Rena didn't give up.
It seemed she wouldn't accept it unless she got inside and confirmed no one was there.
...Watching Rena struggle like that gave me courage.
Maybe they forgot to lock one of the second-floor windows... I'll go check!
Kei-chan, the ladder.
Mion brought over a ladder that had been propped up nearby.
The footing was a little unstable for setting up a ladder, but Mion held it firmly in place.
I hadn't climbed ladders much at all.
...Making my inexperience obvious, I climbed one rung at a time and tried each second-floor window to see if any would open.
Mii-chan, just in case, I'll go check the main house too! I'll be right back!
Rena dashed off. ...What's the main house?
The Furude family's real house. I hear it's been left unattended ever since her parents died.
...Ah... right. ...Rika-chan's parents are... gone, aren't they.
That made me remember again. ...Come to think of it, Satoko lives with her too, right?
Satoko doesn't have parents either. ...They said it was Oyashiro-sama's curse, and her parents fell off a cliff. ...And her older brother, Satoshi-kun... disappeared too...
...Satoshi... I've heard that name before.
I remembered. ...He was the one who disappeared in last year's curse.
Since then, Satoko's been living here in this shack with Rika-chan. Two girls all alone with no family to rely on. ...Helping each other through it.
...You said the main house? It'd be easier to live there...
The curtains kept me from seeing into the room... but for two little girls to live here alone... it looked a little too harsh.
Seems like that's what she did at first. ...But she said it was too painful, because it reminded her of her parents.
...I never knew she bore such a tragic lot in life.
...From how lively she always was at school, there wasn't the slightest hint of any of that...
...Rika-chan too... and Satoko... they've both had it rough.
She's cursed.
...Huh?
I didn't miss how Mion suddenly muttered that in a low voice, like a real curse.
I turned back to Mion below the ladder and asked again.
...Mion... what did you just... say?
I said she's cursed.
...Mion, still bracing the ladder, raised her face to meet my eyes.
...The instant our eyes met, an absolute-zero current shot through my whole body.
Mion's eyes were thick and clouded... and in her pupils, a stew named chaos boiled and churned.
It swirled and swirled... bubbling up in heavy, popping blisters...
...Before I knew it, I'd been cornered in a dead end called a ladder.
...M-Mion... what kind of face... are you making...?
I meant to say it lightly with a strained smile, like, that's going too far for a joke...
...But on that unstable footing, struggling desperately not to be swallowed by the feelings surging up inside me, all I could force out was a hoarse voice.
``Satoko Houjou...@k@vS02/03/130300169. ...She's been cursed by Oyashiro-sama.``
changeReason: Old shortens to 'She's been cursed by Oyashiro-sama'; new preserves 一身に受けた (bore it all by herself) and 呪われた子 (cursed child), adding the concept that Satoko uniquely bears the curse alone

TextENGNew: ...Mion answered that as though replying to someone else's question, even though I hadn't asked.
Only her parents died from that fall off the park observatory. Only those cold parents who treated her like a useless tagalong died, and she alone survived.

...At the home that took her in, the aunt who bullied her was beaten to death by a madman on the night of Watanagashi, her brains splattered to mush, her head battered until it no longer held its shape.
...Even Satoshi-kun, who always protected her... suddenly disappeared on her birthday.
He wasn't one of the people tormenting her, but he was erased anyway. ...The police decided he'd run away from home, but Satoshi-kun wasn't the kind of person who'd run.

He always gave everything he had. He was the kind of person who kept working, little by little, all alone, without leaning on anyone. He worked himself to the bone for his one and only little sister, and still he was made to disappear. He lived for that girl alone, and still he was made to disappear.
Poor Satoshi-kun. Satoshi-kun, never rewarded. How ungrateful that girl is... That girl is cursed.
Whoever gets close to that girl meets the same end. Either die by the curse, or disappear by the curse. There's no peace for that girl, no peace for that girl.
...Rika-chan disappearing was Satoko's fault too, it has to be, it has to be, it has to be, it has to be, it has to be, it has to be, it has to be, it has to b-b-b-b-b...
Mion murmured like someone in delirium... repeating words I could no longer make out.
...Her shoulders shook and trembled, and that trembling ran down the ladder straight into me.
The footing rattled and shook... reminding me just how far away the ground was.
C-calm down, Mion...!! I-I don't even know what you're talking about!!
Whether those words reached Mion's ears was doubtful.
...The hard rattling Mion was causing only seemed to grow worse...
P-...help...
The ladder... sh-she'll knock it over... help... h-help...!!
At that moment, I heard the footsteps of a crowd running toward us.
H-... h-heeey!! H-here, over here!!!!
I was this close to shouting for help.
With Rena in front, four or five adults came rushing over with flashlights in hand.
Keiichi-kun, Mii-chan, sorry we're late! I borrowed the keys!
Oh, that's a relief. Looks like the windows and doors were all locked up tight. We were stuck.
...Hearing Mion say that so casually sent a fresh chill through me.
The one holding the ladder there now was definitely Mion.
Sonozaki Mion herself.
...Then who was it... just a moment ago... muttering like delirium... who was that...?
Who was it... that kept muttering those ominous words about curses...?
...Before Mion could turn into a Mion who wasn't Mion again, I leaped off the ladder as if running for my life.
The adults tried one key after another, trying to open the large shutter on the first floor.
Rika-chan's house, you know, was originally the neighborhood association's disaster storehouse. That's why the village chief's house still keeps the shutter key.
It's Rika-chan and Satoko's home now, though.
...Well, it was on Rika-chan's property to begin with. Nobody complains, though.
Mion says that with a smile in my direction.
...But all I can give her back is an ashen look.
...Mion acting so completely like her usual self only made the eeriness of that not-Mion from before stand out all the more...
Clank... grind, garararararara... KRAAASH!!
The shutter, unopened for ages, was finally pulled up.

Rena felt around for the light switch, found it, and sprang inside.
I followed after her.

Rika-chaaaan!!
Satoko-chaaaan!!
If you're there, answer me!!
We climbed the narrow stairs.
...From outside it looked like nothing but a warehouse, but once we stepped in, it was obvious this was a lived-in home.
...The air was full of a scent that somehow made me think of those two.
Without a doubt, this was Rika-chan and Satoko's house.
The first floor doubled as the town association's storehouse, while the second floor was entirely living space.
Like a one-room apartment, past the kitchen there was a living area about eight tatami mats in size.
Dressers and cupboards were crammed in tight, and in one corner was a heap of laundry that looked freshly brought in.
A folding table sat in the middle of the room, with little bottles of soy sauce and dressing on top... giving off the plain feel of everyday life.
It was strange for both of them to be gone at this hour of the night.
...The adults climbing the stairs one after another began raising their voices all at once.

...Well?
They're still not here?!

Yeah.
...They're not here.
This is definitely strange.
Just yesterday, the village chief disappeared, and the whole village was thrown into an uproar.
...And today, even Rika-chan and Satoko...
...Both of them out roaming this late at night?
That can't be.
But the bicycle was gone.
Where could they have gone at this hour of the night?!

Did they leave and never come back?
Where did they go?!
The adults began voicing one possibility after another, and the room instantly fell into an uproar.
...Before long, Mion brought it under control.

...I don't want to believe it, but the Kimiyoshi village chief disappeared only yesterday.
...We can't say this is unrelated.

...At Mion's blunt conclusion, the adults turned pale...
Rena and I were no different...

For example... they might've gone to a neighbor's place, been treated to tea, and fallen asleep.
Of course, it's also possible they couldn't bring themselves to wake them.
...Let's rule that possibility out first.

Makino-san, please check the houses near the stream from the back side.
Keitarou-san, head toward Ojagaike.
Okamura-san...
Mion briskly gave instructions to the villagers.
...The adults followed Mion's instructions without hesitation, even though she should have been younger than all of them.

I'll stay here and call every likely contact I can think of.
All right, everyone, that's the plan!
Yeah!
The adults answered with a grunt and stomped down the stairs.

...As expected of Mii-chan.
Mii-chan isn't just the class rep at school, you're like Hinamizawa's class rep too!

No teasing, no teasing!
Come on, move.
Oji-san needs to use the phone.
With a proud grin, Mion picked up the receiver and began making calls.
Rena watched dependable Mion with open admiration.
But I... felt conflicted, because Mion's leadership seemed to confirm exactly what Shion and Ooishi-san had said about her being the Sonozaki heir...
...Hey now, Maebara Keiichi.

That doesn't matter right now.
What matters is whether Rika-chan and Satoko are safe!
For all we know... there might be a clue in this room that points to where the two of them went!
...That said... there's no way I could actually know that.

If this room had been ransacked, or if anything had changed drastically from before, maybe I could've reasoned something out,
...but with the room this completely normal, no clue caught my eye.
For one thing... this was my first time visiting this room.
...Without knowing what the room normally looked like, there was no way I could notice anything...
...Hey, don't give up, Maebara Keiichi!

Just search!
For anything!
Grasping for something, I tried opening the dresser and the windows.
...But nothing caught my attention.
Rena, unable to just sit still either, seemed to be scanning the room for clues the same way I was.

...But Rena.
I don't think you're going to find Rika-chan by opening the fridge or the cabinet under the sink.

I thought they might be hiding...
Ha... Hau~...
While we were doing that, things got noisy outside.
...Villagers were gathering one after another, having heard the commotion.
Of course, many of them were probably people Mion had called.

I'll go talk to everyone.
...Sooner or later, the police will show up too.
We'll need to explain the situation to that Ooishi guy.
Mion started heading down the stairs, so we followed her out to the front.

About ten adults had already gathered outside.
...All of them looked uneasy.
Before I knew it, a group of elderly people had gathered near the shrine, rubbing prayer beads as they prayed for Rika-chan's safety.
......This had turned into something serious...
This was no longer just a problem among friends.
Mion-chan, is it true that Rika-chama and Satoko-chan have gone missing?!
The villagers crowded around Mion, forming a ring.
Mion raised her right hand as if to say "calm down," silencing them.
First, let's check every household along the neighborhood association's circulation route and see if they're visiting anyone.
...After all, there's a chance the two of them are sleeping soundly at someone's house, completely unaware of how worried we are.

...It would certainly be hard to wake Rika-chan if she'd eaten her fill and dozed off peacefully like a sleeping kitten.
...If only that were the case.
...I don't want to think about it, but of course, there's also the possibility that something bad has happened.
When Mion said that, her expression turning grimmer than ever, the adults all fell silent...

We'll split up too.
We'll go back around to all the places we searched last night when we were looking for the village chief.

...We'll comb through the dam work site carefully.
You all search around the school.

Right!
Then let's hurry up and get moving!!
Let's go, let's go!!

...Everyone was already up all night searching for the village chief yesterday.
...I know you're all exhausted from lack of sleep, but please, I'm counting on you!
Yeahhh!!!
With that as their signal, the adults scattered in every direction.
Feeling I should head out somewhere too... I started wandering off in a direction no one else had taken.
The signs of other people grew fainter and fainter.
...But I didn't feel particularly scared.
...If anyone was going to be made to disappear, it should be me or Shion first.
So by all rights, I should have been more afraid of the darkness.

...And yet I wasn't afraid.
...Why?
Because tonight... someone had already disappeared.
...So no one else would disappear tonight...
Before I could feel guilty about the basis for that selfish sense of security... I, frantic and utterly spent, would need a little more time............
